task:
Open /home/user/Documents/employees.xlsx in LibreOffice Calc. The 'Employees' sheet has bold headers in row 1 (A='EmployeeID', B='Name', C='Department', D='Salary', E='HireDate') and 20 employees in rows 2-21; exactly 5 of them have Salary > 80000.

Do all of the following:
- On the 'Employees' sheet, enable AutoFilter so filter dropdowns appear on the header row.
- On the 'Employees' sheet, freeze the top row so row 1 stays visible while scrolling.
- Add a new sheet named exactly 'HighEarners'. Copy the header row from Employees A1:E1 into HighEarners A1:E1, then copy every employee row with Salary (column D) > 80000 into HighEarners starting at row 2. HighEarners must have exactly 5 data rows (rows 2-6).
- Save the HighEarners sheet as a CSV at /home/user/Documents/high_earners.csv. The CSV must have exactly 6 lines (1 header + 5 data rows), be UTF-8 encoded, and use comma as the field separator.
- Save the original .xlsx back to /home/user/Documents/employees.xlsx.

verification.eval: len(result.get('data', [])) == 5 and all(isinstance(row[3], (int, float)) and row[3] > 80000 for row in result.get('data', []))
verification.description: HighEarners A2:D6 has 5 rows, all with Salary > 80000
